title: Orley Vanicus
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Character |type=Galactic Republic |image=[[File:Vanicus.jpg]] |name=Orley Vanicus |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]][[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 2|''Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith'' 2]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]][[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 2|''Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War'' 2]] |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes=[[Color/Legends|Brown]][[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 6|''Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War'' 6]] |skin= |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=*[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] **[[Republic Navy/Legends|Republic Navy]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Battle stations! Fire on my signal!|Admiral Orley Vanicus|}} [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] '''Orley Vanicus''' was considered a hero of the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]]. A member of the [[Republic Navy/Legends|Republic Navy]] and [[Captain/Legends|captain]] of the [[Command ship/Legends|command ship]] [[Reliance I|''Reliance'']] over [[4000 BBY/Legends|four thousand]] years before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], he was a simple man who didn't dabble in politics or bureaucracy and stuck with what he specialized at: commanding [[Starship/Legends|starships]]. During the [[Krath Holy Crusade|Krath conflict]], he commanded a [[Battle of Koros Major|mission]] to the [[Empress Teta system/Legends|Empress Teta system]] alongside [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]] and [[Nomi Sunrider]], with the intention of protecting [[Empress Teta/Legends|Koros Major]] from the [[Krath/Legends|Krath]] faction. Vanicus and his men were too late, though, and the mission was a failure; they were tricked by the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] [[Mind trick/Legends|illusions]] of [[Sith sorcerer|Sith sorceress]] [[Aleema Keto]], and [[Death/Legends|suicide]] Krath [[CX-133 Chaos fighter|Chaos fighters]] pummeled the ''Reliance'' and other ships, until Vanicus ordered a retreat. [[3996 BBY|Later]], during the Great Sith War, he was dispatched to the [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyards]] at [[Foerost/Legends|Foerost]] with the intention of intercepting a [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] group that had been raiding the Republic for weeks. His forces were too late, arriving to find that the majority of the ships under construction at the facility had been stolen and the defensive fleet stationed there routed. He and his staff salvaged what they could, and through a security recording in one of the shipyard's control centers, Vanicus discovered that the pirates' leader was Ulic Qel-Droma, who had left the Republic and joined the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]]. Acting on planted intelligence which Qel-Droma hoped would lure the Republic away from their capital of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], Vanicus sent the majority of the Republic Navy to [[Kemplex Nine]], believing that Qel-Droma's forces were gathered there. Qel-Droma and his [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] allies subsequently [[Battle of Coruscant (Great Sith War)|launched an attack]] on the defenseless Coruscant, with Qel-Droma's fellow [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]] slaying [[Sidrona|the Supreme Chancellor]] and [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Vodo-Siosk Baas]]. Later in the war, Vanicus led a joint Republic/[[Onderon/Legends|Onderonian]] force in [[Battle of Onderon (Great Sith War)|battle]] against [[Mandalore the Indomitable]], driving them back to the wild jungle [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of [[Dxun/Legends|Dxun]], where most of the Mandalorians were killed by native predators. After the Sith War, Vanicus was considered something of a war hero, and continued to serve the Republic Navy for a time. ==Biography== ===A military man=== Orley Vanicus, a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]],''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]'' was a member of the [[Vanicus family]], known for its influential political dynasty during the early time of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]].{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/source/praji/20090205.html|text=Tinker, Tailor, Soldier, Praji|int=Tinker, Tailor, Soldier, Praji}} As of [[4000 BBY/Legends|four thousand]] years before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]],''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' he was an [[Admiral/Legends|admiral]] in the [[Republic Navy/Legends|Republic Navy]], [[Captain/Legends|captain]] of the [[Republic command ship]] [[Reliance I|''Reliance'']]. During this period, admirals were informally known as "[[Fleet Captain/Legends|fleet captains]]," and Vanicus was often referred to solely as "captain," despite being of a higher rank. His early career occurred in a time of relative peace, where the Republic Navy—considered by many to be antiquated and pointless—had little to do bar basic peacekeeping activities, and had only a limited fleet. ===Krath conflict=== {{Quote|Looks like we're too late to save Koros Major, Ulic—''"
"''Perhaps, Captain Vanicus. Transmit your cease fire order…Then we'll see if the new Teta leaders have any respect for ''galactic law''!|Vanicus, and Ulic Qel-Droma|}} [[File:Reliance.jpg|thumb|left|220px|The ''Reliance'' enters the Empress Teta system.]] In [[3997 BBY]], the [[Krath/Legends|Krath]] faction of the [[Empress Teta system/Legends|Empress Teta system]] launched a [[Political uprising of the Empress Teta system|violent political coup]] against the Tetan government, with Krath leaders [[Satal Keto/Legends|Satal]] and [[Aleema Keto]] installing themselves as the [[Star system/Legends|system's]] new leaders. They conquered all of the planets in the system bar the capital, [[Empress Teta/Legends|Koros Major]], which put up a stern resistance. Both the Republic and the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] were concerned by the actions of the Krath, and arranged for a joint Republic/Jedi peacekeeping fleet, led by Vanicus, to [[Battle of Koros Major|strike back]] at the Krath and stop the Ketos from taking Koros Major. In addition to a number of Jedi serving through the fleet, [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]] and [[Nomi Sunrider]] accompanied Vanicus on his [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] for the mission in an advisory capacity. Vanicus was itching for a fight, ready to do damage to the Krath. The fleet, consisting of numerous [[Praetorian-class frigate|''Praetorian''-class frigates]], [[Destroyer/Legends|escort destroyers]], [[Cruiser/Legends|heavy cruisers]], and myriad other vessels, exited [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] and arrived in the system shortly after Koros Major had fallen. Vanicus remarked to his two Jedi advisors that they had arrived too late to complete their mission, though Qel-Droma had him issue an order to cease fire regardless, hoping to test the Krath's resolve when faced with orders from the Galactic Republic. While they waited for a response, Vanicus had the Republic fleet surround the Krath command station. No response came, though; instead, a number of behemoth, long-thought-extinct creatures known as [[space grazer]]s materialized, and began preying on Vanicus's vulnerable fleet. The Republic fleet was perplexed by the creatures' sudden appearance, as space grazers were the stuff of legend; Nomi Sunrider quickly discerned, though, that they were mere [[Mind trick/Legends|illusions]] created by [[Sith sorcery/Legends|Sith magic]]. Realizing that the numbers of the Krath forces were swollen with many such [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] illusions, Vanicus and the fleet stormed the Krath command station, dealing it significant damage. The Krath were quick to counterattack, launching hordes of [[CX-133 Chaos fighter]]s at the ''Reliance''. Vanicus began to make the order to fire at them in return, though was cut off by Nomi Sunrider, who believed it was another illusion. She used [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to stop Aleema Keto, who was conjuring the illusions, but had miscalculated; though the majority of the Chaos fighters were false, many were real, hidden among the illusory vessels. The [[Death/Legends|suicide]] fighters rammed the ''Reliance'' and many other ships, causing immense casualties for the Republic. The canopy of Vanicus's flagship was breached, and the bridge was damaged, with Ulic Qel-Droma sustaining an injury in the attack. However, the ''Reliance'' was still intact, and Vanicus, believing that the entire operation had to be re-planned, ordered an immediate retreat. ===Ruse at Kemplex Nine=== {{Quote|Dispatch all ships ''immediately'' to Kemplex Nine! We'll catch him this time! Now I remember—he is a Jedi! Our enemy is Ulic Qel-Droma! We must report this to Coruscant at once. This is a dark day for the Republic.|Vanicus, at Foerost|}} [[File:Vanicus_at_Foerost.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Vanicus discovers that Ulic Qel-Droma was the leader of the attack on Foerost.]] In [[3996 BBY]], a number of months after the fleet's botched attempt to protect Koros Major, Admiral Vanicus was dispatched to the [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyards]] of the planet [[Foerost/Legends|Foerost]], located in the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]]. Foerost, one of the oldest and most successful [[Starship/Legends|starship]] construction facilities in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], had been the latest target in a series of raids perpetrated by what the Republic believed was a highly-organized group of [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]], though rumors abounded that a Jedi had a hand in the attacks. Because of Foerost's importance, Vanicus took the main Republic fleet there post haste, hoping to catch the marauders in the act, discern who they were, and put an end to them with the Republic's superior firepower. Once again, however, Vanicus found that he was too late. The fleet arrived to find the remnants of Foerost's defensive fleet, which had been soundly defeated by the raiders. The shipyards had been pillaged, with few intact ships remaining. Vanicus's fleet was thoroughly unprepared for the aftermath of such a rout, and the admiral discerned that whoever was behind the attacks had far stronger firepower than the Republic had been led to believe. Although his fleet was not well-suited to it, Vanicus had the forces under his command sift through the wreckage to salvage what they could, and to rescue any survivors. Meanwhile, Vanicus personally inspected the shipyards' various control centers with his staff, hoping to find any survivors who had witnessed the attack and could identify the pirates. However, few [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pods]] had been jettisoned from the command center, with even fewer conscious survivors. Desperation setting in, Vanicus ordered his men to search through the station's logs and security records, though they reported back that most computer systems had been sabotaged by the attackers. Eventually, however, one man located an intact recording that had avoided the pirates' purge. It was a [[Hologram/Legends|hologram]] recording, depicting a young man speaking to a subordinate—Vanicus quickly recognized the speaker as Ulic Qel-Droma, whom he had fought alongside at the Battle of Koros; the young Jedi had turned to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] and become a [[Sith/Legends|Sith]], assembling an armada in cohesion with Aleema Keto and the Krath. Qel-Droma ordered his subordinate to take his fleet to the rendezvous point for an impending attack on [[Kemplex Nine]], a strategically-located [[Space station/Legends|space station]] that was completely undefended. Shocked by the news that the Republic's enemy was a Jedi, Vanicus ordered the bulk of the Republic Navy to travel immediately to Kemplex Nine, to finally catch Qel-Droma and end his piracy. Vanicus immediately sent a message to [[Minister of Defense (Republic)|Minister of Defense]] [[Netus]] on Coruscant, who relayed it to [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Vodo-Siosk Baas]]. Vanicus's fleet went straight to Kemplex Nine, and was soon joined by additional Republic forces; they laid in wait for the Sith attack, but it never came. It had been a feint; Qel-Droma had purposely allowed Vanicus to uncover the recording in order to drive Republic forces away from Coruscant, where he launched his [[Battle of Coruscant (Great Sith War)|true attack]]. ===Vanquishing the Mandalorians=== {{Quote|Captain Vanicus, here…Republic reinforcements have arrived as requested. Mandalorian warriors, surrender! Your lord, Ulic Qel-Droma, is defeated! Your cause is lost!|Vanicus, to Mandalore the Indomitable|}} Vanicus's fleet left Kemplex Nine but was too late to do anything for Coruscant; Qel-Droma's [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] ally, [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]], murdered Jedi Master Vodo-Siosk Baas and [[Sidrona|the Supreme Chancellor]] in the [[Senate Hall]]. However, in the subsequent weeks, the Republic managed to turn the tide on Kun and Qel-Droma in what was later known as the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]]. Ulic Qel-Droma was defeated and [[Sever Force/Legends|stripped]] of the Force on [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]], and he gave the Republic the co-ordinates of Kun's secret base on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]]. Meanwhile, Vanicus and the remainder of the Navy were sent to [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]], where the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Crusader (Victory-class)|Crusaders]], Kun's allies, were [[Battle of Onderon (Great Sith War)|in battle]] with the local Onderonian [[Beast Riders]], led by [[Oron Kira]]. [[File:Battle_of_Onderon_GSW.jpg|thumb|left|230px|Vanicus' fleet arrives at Onderon.]] Vanicus and his forces arrived in-system to find Onderon overrun with Mandalorians, led by [[Mandalore the Indomitable]], over the planet's capital city, [[Iziz/Legends|Iziz]]. Vanicus issued the Mandalorians with an ultimatum, telling them that their leader, Qel-Droma, was defeated and that they had no hope of triumphing against the Republic's superior force. He ordered them to surrender immediately, though Mandalore the Indomitable refused, decreeing that the Mandalorians would fight to the death. Vanicus's and Oron Kira's forces slagged their enemies' warships and began to drive them back, thinning their numbers ''en masse'', but because of the Mandalorians' advanced [[Deflector shield/Legends|deflector shields]], finishing them off without excessive firepower was nearly impossible. Vanicus authorized the use of extreme force against Mandalore; before long, the enemy battleships were all destroyed and their [[Basilisk war droid/Legends|Basilisk war droids]] were decimated. Onderon's atmosphere periodically merged with that of its jungle [[Moon/Legends|moon]], [[Dxun/Legends|Dxun]], and at the time of the battle the two worlds were linked this way. The remaining Mandalorians on war droids were able to exploit this, retreating toward the moon without having to enter [[Realspace/Legends|space]]. Vanicus and the beast riders pursued them, picking off the Mandalorians one by one. Many of the Mandalorians' mounts, including that of Mandalore the Indomitable, managed to put a sizable distance between them and the Republic/Onderonian force, and Vanicus reluctantly called off the Republic attack. He and his staff hoped that Dxun's fauna—considered among the most fierce in the galaxy—would finish off the surviving Mandalorians. This was the case, with many Crusaders, including Mandalore the Indomitable, perishing, but some survived, and the [[Mandalore (title)/Legends|Mandalore]] tradition lived on. The Republic was [[Battle of Yavin 4 (Great Sith War)|successful]] against Kun on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]], and the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] was defeated. The Great Sith War was over. ===War hero=== {{Quote|…[like] running a protection racket.|Vanicus' thoughts on the Commercial Protectorate Acts|}} After the Great Sith War, Orley Vanicus was considered a war hero for his part in defeating the Krath, the Sith, and the Mandalorians. He continued to serve for a number of years after the conflict's end,''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic Handbook]]'' states that Saul Karath began his naval career on Vanicus' vessel, beginning a career that spanned thirty years. As the ''Knights of the Old Republic Handbook'' is set during the Mandalorian Wars almost forty years after the Great Sith War, Vanicus would have to have still been a naval officer for at least six years watching as the Republic and its Navy underwent much reform. The Navy was reformed and became a stronger, less tradition-bound organization, while a number of [[Commercial Protectorate Acts]] were also passed, decreeing that the Navy be given more resources in exchange for protecting lucrative Republic trade routes. Vanicus was openly critical of the Acts, comparing the Republic's actions to illegal protection rackets. Some years after the Sith War, Vanicus's eye was caught by a young [[Corellia/Legends|Corellian]] named [[Saul Karath]], who was attempting to join the Navy but had neither the connections nor the years required to gain any sort of position. Vanicus allowed Karath join his crew as a [[Steward (occupation)/Legends|cabin steward]],''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic Handbook]]'' and after an act of heroism, recommended him to another captain, leading to Karath's first commission; his first in a career that spanned over three decades and earned him admiralty. Vanicus's concerns about the Commercial Protectorate Acts was not unfounded, and they proved the Navy's downfall in the early stages of the [[Mandalorian Wars]]. Saul Karath became an important Republic figure in that conflict, but ultimately turned his back on the Republic and sided with the Sith during the [[Jedi Civil War]]. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|…we are too late. We were unprepared to see the aftermath of such a great battle. Most of the ships have been stolen, the facilities destroyed. We must salvage what we can…and learn who has done this thing!|Vanicus|}} [[File:Vanicus_at_Onderon.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Vanicus commanding his fleet during the Great Sith War.]] Vanicus was a man who relished battle and served the Republic loyally; he didn't care for politics or money, and openly criticized aspects of how the Navy was run in the aftermath of the Sith War. He was a proud citizen of the Galactic Republic and had no criticisms of the Jedi, serving alongside them on occasion during his career. When he discovered Ulic Qel-Droma had been behind the raid on Foerost, he made no attempt to hide his shock from his crew, remarking that it was a dark day for the Republic. Vanicus was eager to help the Republic war effort during the Sith War, though this was sometimes taken advantage of—were it not for the advice of the Jedi, he would have committed his forces to attacking Aleema Keto's Force apparitions, and, later, Qel-Droma was able to trick him into going to Kemplex Nine. Vanicus was decisive, and he did not delay before making an order; after discovering Qel-Droma's planted evidence at Foerost, he immediately had most of the Republic Navy travel to intercept the Sith and Mandalorians at Kemplex Nine. He was also a merciful man, allowing the Mandalorians the option of surrendering during the Battle of Onderon. He had great perseverance, refusing to give up in his efforts to find out who was leading the attacks on the Republic after Foerost, and meticulous: he had his men comb even inch of the space station searching for security records. In his later years, Vanicus became a famous veteran of the Sith War, and was often outspoken in his opinions on the changing Republic; he was unhappy that [[Galactic economy|commerce]] controlled the Republic, and made no attempt to hide his contempt for the Commercial Protectorate Acts. He also had an eye for young talent in this time, noticing Saul Karath, who eventually became a revered Republic admiral. ==Behind the scenes== [[File:Oldrepublic_crest.svg|thumb|right|150px|Symbol on Vanicus' helmet]] Vanicus was created by [[Kevin J. Anderson]] and [[Tom Veitch]] for the [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi (comic series)|''Tales of the Jedi'']] comic series. He first appeared in the [[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 2|second issue]] of the [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith|''Dark Lords of the Sith'']] story arc, and later reappeared in the [[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 2|second]] and [[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 6|final issues]] of the [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith|''Dark Lords of the Sith'']] arc. Vanicus is also featured in the [[Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith (audio drama)|''Dark Lords of the Sith'' audio dramatization]]. He received a short entry in [[Stephen J. Sansweet's]] ''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' in [[1998]], but was not used again in [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] until [[John Jackson Miller]] mentioned him in the [[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic Handbook|''Knights of the Old Republic Handbook'']], tying ''Tales of the Jedi'' into the [[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic (comic series)|''Knights of the Old Republic'']] comic series. He was again reused almost a year later in [[August]] [[2008]] in the ''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]'', which provided him with a first name, "Orley." The ''Campaign Guide'' also introduced a [[Retroactive continuity|retcon]] that "fleet captain," Vanicus's stated rank in ''Tales of the Jedi'', was an informal nickname for the rank of admiral, and that Vanicus had in fact been an admiral during the events of ''Tales of the Jedi''. In [[2009]], a [[Vanicus]] family was mentioned in the [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|Hyperspace]] article ''[[Tinker, Tailor, Soldier, Praji]]'', and [[Nathan O'Keefe]], the author of the article, claimed that he intended the family to be a reference to Orley Vanicus.{{Blog|valinkenobi|14/comments|TTSP Endnotes: Volume 1}} He was later given an entry in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''. ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Orley Vanicus}} *[[Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith (audio drama)|''Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith'' audio drama]] *[[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 2|''Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith'' 2]] {{1st}} *[[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 2|''Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War'' 2]] *[[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 6|''Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War'' 6]] ==Sources== *{{InsiderCite|28|The Horse's Mouth|Straight from the Horse's Mouth: A Guide to the ''Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi'' Universe, Part 3}} *''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic Handbook]]'' *''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]'' {{1stID|Orley}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |es=Vanicus }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Vanicus, Orley}} [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Republic Navy captains]]
